revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:合氣神社, en:Iwama Dōjō); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == == 外部リンク修正 == 編集者の皆さんこんにちは、 「[[合氣神社]]」上の1個の外部リンクを修正しました。[https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=65719812 今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.htmlにアーカイブ（https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html）を追加 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2017年9月28日 (木) 10:05 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == {{old prod full}} {{Talk header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Martial arts}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Low}} }} == Focus of article == The focus of this article is very much on the Iwama style/Aikikai controversy. There must be many other things to say about the dojo; if the original buildings are maintained, and the history of life in the dojo both before and after osensei's death. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) == Merge? == I suggest we merge [[Aiki Jinja]] into this article. Sure the dojo and the shrine are separate entities, but still all the aikido buildings in Iwama and what has been happening around them is one and the same concept to most people. Having them as separate articles means info has to been repeated in both articles, if the stories are to be understandable to others than insiders. I am not sure if "Iwama dojo" is the best title for that article though, but I can not think of any better. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Please try - I did a bit a ways back trying to make all more neutral and sounding less like a glee club but was afraid to do too much at once, especailly since the split with Aikikai was so recent. There was no controversy over the changes I did so I suspect that now is a good time to attempt it. I feel that either the Iwama Ryu and Iwama Style articles were started by somone who did not search for what was already there. Good luck and I'm looking forward to your change.[[User:PRehse|Peter Rehse]] 06:19, 22 February 2006 (UTC) == De-merge? == I just started writing an article on Aiki Jinja because for some reason it seemed the Wikipedia search engine couldn't find it. A subsequent search turned this up. I can't understand why the dojo and shrine articles have been merged into one. They are two things, although very closely related, and should have their own separate articles. I disagree that it would be hard for other than insiders to understand the relationship between them, and that it would require repetition. With well-written entries, it would work well this way. I would support splitting them back up again, but don't want to just barge in and do it unless others agree (I see by this history that the "unopposed merge" happened very recently!) If what I see here indicates that I'm not the only one who thinks this article is not "encyclopedic" enough (glee club? :-), then let's make it more so. Also I would make more salient mention of "hono embu" and less of lunch :-) Finally, just scanning over the dojo article...final line: Tai Sai on ''May'' 29th??? This should be April, right? They don't have another one a month later, do they? Geezis, does anybody pay attention to what they're writing!? [[User:Djiann|Djiann]] 01:17, 5 November 2006 (UTC) :I agree with the "de-merging" proposal. --[[User:GenkiNeko|GenkiNeko]] 04:23, 24 November 2006 (UTC) :: As long as there was fairly little information on the shrine, it made sense to merge them rather than the article of the shrine being a short stub mainly consisting of information that also could be found in the other article. Whoever has material for articles with some substance on both subjects, please go ahead. // [[User:Habj|habj]] 18:47, 25 November 2006 (UTC) == different dojo == Isn't it the case that Aiki Jinja and Ibaraki branch dojo are located on opposite sides of the road, and that each side has a building with a tatami-floored training space (i.e., two dojos, even before including the Saito dojo and others)? A recent anon edit has changed the wording to say that ibaraki branch dojo itself is the one dojo contained in aiki jinja. Perhaps someone has a better source, but this [http://www.guillaumeerard.com/aikido/events/79-the-yearly-celebration-of-the-aiki-jinja-taisai] seems to show two distinct tatami areas used for aikido. [[User:Cesiumfrog|Cesiumfrog]] ([[User talk:Cesiumfrog|talk]]) 06:10, 30 November 2012 (UTC) — I did the edit. Please feel free to modify it to clarify: Erard's blog shows only one dojo. The other tatami area is the actual Haiden of the Aiki Shrine. If you look closely you can see normal straw tatami and the kamidana. This area is not used for training. About once a month the Haiden is used for Tsukinamisai (a shinto ceremony)and worship (no aikido) and once a year Doshu and Waka Sensei do a demonstration offering (hono embu) of aikido as part of Taisai. That is what the other picture shows in the blog. The demonstration is offered at the shrine much like the sea bream and vegetables on the kamidana. The only time the shrine has been used for actual training was when the dojo was damaged by the Tohoku Earthquake. The straw tatami were soon shredded and later had to be replaced . [[Special:Contributions/202.232.52.138|202.232.52.138]] ([[User talk:202.232.52.138|talk]]) 00:03, 19 December 2012 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified 6 external links on [[Iwama dojo]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=775649549 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110317005308/http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ to http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html to http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110706100816/http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html to http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110512081805/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20071013161023/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20121105041433/http://aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm to http://www.aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 06:51, 16 April 2017 (UTC)

page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == 外部リンク修正 == 編集者の皆さんこんにちは、 「[[合氣神社]]」上の1個の外部リンクを修正しました。[https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=65719812 今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.htmlにアーカイブ（https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html）を追加 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2017年9月28日 (木) 10:05 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1207763549 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{old prod full}} {{Talk header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Martial arts}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Low}} }} == Focus of article == The focus of this article is very much on the Iwama style/Aikikai controversy. There must be many other things to say about the dojo; if the original buildings are maintained, and the history of life in the dojo both before and after osensei's death. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Merge? == I suggest we merge [[Aiki Jinja]] into this article. Sure the dojo and the shrine are separate entities, but still all the aikido buildings in Iwama and what has been happening around them is one and the same concept to most people. Having them as separate articles means info has to been repeated in both articles, if the stories are to be understandable to others than insiders. I am not sure if "Iwama dojo" is the best title for that article though, but I can not think of any better. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Please try - I did a bit a ways back trying to make all more neutral and sounding less like a glee club but was afraid to do too much at once, especailly since the split with Aikikai was so recent. There was no controversy over the changes I did so I suspect that now is a good time to attempt it. I feel that either the Iwama Ryu and Iwama Style articles were started by somone who did not search for what was already there. Good luck and I'm looking forward to your change.[[User:PRehse|Peter Rehse]] 06:19, 22 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == De-merge? == I just started writing an article on Aiki Jinja because for some reason it seemed the Wikipedia search engine couldn't find it. A subsequent search turned this up. I can't understand why the dojo and shrine articles have been merged into one. They are two things, although very closely related, and should have their own separate articles. I disagree that it would be hard for other than insiders to understand the relationship between them, and that it would require repetition. With well-written entries, it would work well this way. I would support splitting them back up again, but don't want to just barge in and do it unless others agree (I see by this history that the "unopposed merge" happened very recently!) If what I see here indicates that I'm not the only one who thinks this article is not "encyclopedic" enough (glee club? :-), then let's make it more so. Also I would make more salient mention of "hono embu" and less of lunch :-) Finally, just scanning over the dojo article...final line: Tai Sai on ''May'' 29th??? This should be April, right? They don't have another one a month later, do they? Geezis, does anybody pay attention to what they're writing!? [[User:Djiann|Djiann]] 01:17, 5 November 2006 (UTC) :I agree with the "de-merging" proposal. --[[User:GenkiNeko|GenkiNeko]] 04:23, 24 November 2006 (UTC) :: As long as there was fairly little information on the shrine, it made sense to merge them rather than the article of the shrine being a short stub mainly consisting of information that also could be found in the other article. Whoever has material for articles with some substance on both subjects, please go ahead. // [[User:Habj|habj]] 18:47, 25 November 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == different dojo == Isn't it the case that Aiki Jinja and Ibaraki branch dojo are located on opposite sides of the road, and that each side has a building with a tatami-floored training space (i.e., two dojos, even before including the Saito dojo and others)? A recent anon edit has changed the wording to say that ibaraki branch dojo itself is the one dojo contained in aiki jinja. Perhaps someone has a better source, but this [http://www.guillaumeerard.com/aikido/events/79-the-yearly-celebration-of-the-aiki-jinja-taisai] seems to show two distinct tatami areas used for aikido. [[User:Cesiumfrog|Cesiumfrog]] ([[User talk:Cesiumfrog|talk]]) 06:10, 30 November 2012 (UTC) — I did the edit. Please feel free to modify it to clarify: Erard's blog shows only one dojo. The other tatami area is the actual Haiden of the Aiki Shrine. If you look closely you can see normal straw tatami and the kamidana. This area is not used for training. About once a month the Haiden is used for Tsukinamisai (a shinto ceremony)and worship (no aikido) and once a year Doshu and Waka Sensei do a demonstration offering (hono embu) of aikido as part of Taisai. That is what the other picture shows in the blog. The demonstration is offered at the shrine much like the sea bream and vegetables on the kamidana. The only time the shrine has been used for actual training was when the dojo was damaged by the Tohoku Earthquake. The straw tatami were soon shredded and later had to be replaced . [[Special:Contributions/202.232.52.138|202.232.52.138]] ([[User talk:202.232.52.138|talk]]) 00:03, 19 December 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified 6 external links on [[Iwama dojo]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=775649549 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110317005308/http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ to http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html to http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110706100816/http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html to http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110512081805/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20071013161023/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20121105041433/http://aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm to http://www.aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 06:51, 16 April 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:43, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

Whoever has material for articles with some substance on both subjects, please go ahead. // [[User:Habj|habj]] 18:47, 25 November 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == different dojo == Isn't it the case that Aiki Jinja and Ibaraki branch dojo are located on opposite sides of the road, and that each side has a building with a tatami-floored training space (i.e., two dojos, even before including the Saito dojo and others)? A recent anon edit has changed the wording to say that ibaraki branch dojo itself is the one dojo contained in aiki jinja. Perhaps someone has a better source, but this [http://www.guillaumeerard.com/aikido/events/79-the-yearly-celebration-of-the-aiki-jinja-taisai] seems to show two distinct tatami areas used for aikido. [[User:Cesiumfrog|Cesiumfrog]] ([[User talk:Cesiumfrog|talk]]) 06:10, 30 November 2012 (UTC) — I did the edit. Please feel free to modify it to clarify: Erard's blog shows only one dojo. The other tatami area is the actual Haiden of the Aiki Shrine. If you look closely you can see normal straw tatami and the kamidana. This area is not used for training. About once a month the Haiden is used for Tsukinamisai (a shinto ceremony)and worship (no aikido) and once a year Doshu and Waka Sensei do a demonstration offering (hono embu) of aikido as part of Taisai. That is what the other picture shows in the blog. The demonstration is offered at the shrine much like the sea bream and vegetables on the kamidana. The only time the shrine has been used for actual training was when the dojo was damaged by the Tohoku Earthquake. The straw tatami were soon shredded and later had to be replaced . [[Special:Contributions/202.232.52.138|202.232.52.138]] ([[User talk:202.232.52.138|talk]]) 00:03, 19 December 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified 6 external links on [[Iwama dojo]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=775649549 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110317005308/http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ to http://www.aikidojournal.com/blog/2011/03/14/news-from-iwama-following-the-japan-earthquake-by-stanley-pranin/ *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html to http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110706100816/http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html to http://www.aiki.com.au/Hitohiro/index.html *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20110512081805/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article?articleID=62 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20071013161023/http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 to http://www.aikidojournal.com/article.php?articleID=102 *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20121105041433/http://aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm to http://www.aikikai.or.jp/eng/ibaragi/ibaragi.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 06:51, 16 April 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:30, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

今回の編集]の確認にご協力お願いします。もし何か疑問点がある場合、もしくはリンクや記事をボットの処理対象から外す必要がある場合は、こちらの[[meta:InternetArchiveBot/FAQ|FAQ]]をご覧ください。以下の通り編集しました。 *http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.htmlにアーカイブ（https://web.archive.org/web/20110719092856/http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html）を追加 編集の確認が終わりましたら、下記のテンプレートの指示にしたがってURLの問題を修正してください。 ありがとうございました。—[[:en:User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[:en:User talk:InternetArchiveBot|バグを報告する]]) 2017年9月28日 (木) 10:05 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1207763549 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{old prod full}} {{Talk header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Martial arts}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Low}} }} == Focus of article == The focus of this article is very much on the Iwama style/Aikikai controversy. There must be many other things to say about the dojo; if the original buildings are maintained, and the history of life in the dojo both before and after osensei's death. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Merge? == I suggest we merge [[Aiki Jinja]] into this article. Sure the dojo and the shrine are separate entities, but still all the aikido buildings in Iwama and what has been happening around them is one and the same concept to most people. Having them as separate articles means info has to been repeated in both articles, if the stories are to be understandable to others than insiders. I am not sure if "Iwama dojo" is the best title for that article though, but I can not think of any better. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Please try - I did a bit a ways back trying to make all more neutral and sounding less like a glee club but was afraid to do too much at once, especailly since the split with Aikikai was so recent. There was no controversy over the changes I did so I suspect that now is a good time to attempt it. I feel that either the Iwama Ryu and Iwama Style articles were started by somone who did not search for what was already there. Good luck and I'm looking forward to your change.[[User:PRehse|Peter Rehse]] 06:19, 22 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == De-merge? == I just started writing an article on Aiki Jinja because for some reason it seemed the Wikipedia search engine couldn't find it. A subsequent search turned this up. I can't understand why the dojo and shrine articles have been merged into one. They are two things, although very closely related, and should have their own separate articles. I disagree that it would be hard for other than insiders to understand the relationship between them, and that it would require repetition. With well-written entries, it would work well this way. I would support splitting them back up again, but don't want to just barge in and do it unless others agree (I see by this history that the "unopposed merge" happened very recently!) If what I see here indicates that I'm not the only one who thinks this article is not "encyclopedic" enough (glee club? :-), then let's make it more so. Also I would make more salient mention of "hono embu" and less of lunch :-) Finally, just scanning over the dojo article...final line: Tai Sai on ''May'' 29th??? This should be April, right? They don't have another one a month later, do they? Geezis, does anybody pay attention to what they're writing!? [[User:Djiann|Djiann]] 01:17, 5 November 2006 (UTC) :I agree with the "de-merging" proposal. --[[User:GenkiNeko|GenkiNeko]] 04:23, 24 November 2006 (UTC) :: As long as there was fairly little information on the shrine, it made sense to merge them rather than the article of the shrine being a short stub mainly consisting of information that also could be found in the other article. Whoever has material for articles with some substance on both subjects, please go ahead. // [[User:Habj|habj]] 18:47, 25 November 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == different dojo == Isn't it the case that Aiki Jinja and Ibaraki branch dojo are located on opposite sides of the road, and that each side has a building with a tatami-floored training space (i.e., two dojos, even before including the Saito dojo and others)? A recent anon edit has changed the wording to say that ibaraki branch dojo itself is the one dojo contained in aiki jinja. Perhaps someone has a better source, but this [http://www.guillaumeerard.com/aikido/events/79-the-yearly-celebration-of-the-aiki-jinja-taisai] seems to show two distinct tatami areas used for aikido. [[User:Cesiumfrog|Cesiumfrog]] ([[User talk:Cesiumfrog|talk]]) 06:10, 30 November 2012 (UTC) — I did the edit. Please feel free to modify it to clarify: Erard's blog shows only one dojo. The other tatami area is the actual Haiden of the Aiki Shrine. If you look closely you can see normal straw tatami and the kamidana. This area is not used for training. About once a month the Haiden is used for Tsukinamisai (a shinto ceremony)and worship (no aikido) and once a year Doshu and Waka Sensei do a demonstration offering (hono embu) of aikido as part of Taisai. That is what the other picture shows in the blog. The demonstration is offered at the shrine much like the sea bream and vegetables on the kamidana. The only time the shrine has been used for actual training was when the dojo was damaged by the Tohoku Earthquake. The straw tatami were soon shredded and later had to be replaced . ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{old prod full}} {{Talk page header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Martial arts}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Low}} }} == Focus of article == The focus of this article is very much on the Iwama style/Aikikai controversy. There must be many other things to say about the dojo; if the original buildings are maintained, and the history of life in the dojo both before and after osensei's death. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Merge? == I suggest we merge [[Aiki Jinja]] into this article. Sure the dojo and the shrine are separate entities, but still all the aikido buildings in Iwama and what has been happening around them is one and the same concept to most people. Having them as separate articles means info has to been repeated in both articles, if the stories are to be understandable to others than insiders. I am not sure if "Iwama dojo" is the best title for that article though, but I can not think of any better. // [[User:Habj|Habj]] 21:54, 21 February 2006 (UTC) :Please try - I did a bit a ways back trying to make all more neutral and sounding less like a glee club but was afraid to do too much at once, especailly since the split with Aikikai was so recent. There was no controversy over the changes I did so I suspect that now is a good time to attempt it. I feel that either the Iwama Ryu and Iwama Style articles were started by somone who did not search for what was already there. Good luck and I'm looking forward to your change.[[User:PRehse|Peter Rehse]] 06:19, 22 February 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == De-merge? == I just started writing an article on Aiki Jinja because for some reason it seemed the Wikipedia search engine couldn't find it. A subsequent search turned this up. I can't understand why the dojo and shrine articles have been merged into one. They are two things, although very closely related, and should have their own separate articles. I disagree that it would be hard for other than insiders to understand the relationship between them, and that it would require repetition. With well-written entries, it would work well this way. I would support splitting them back up again, but don't want to just barge in and do it unless others agree (I see by this history that the "unopposed merge" happened very recently!) If what I see here indicates that I'm not the only one who thinks this article is not "encyclopedic" enough (glee club? :-), then let's make it more so. Also I would make more salient mention of "hono embu" and less of lunch :-) Finally, just scanning over the dojo article...final line: Tai Sai on ''May'' 29th??? This should be April, right? They don't have another one a month later, do they? Geezis, does anybody pay attention to what they're writing!? [[User:Djiann|Djiann]] 01:17, 5 November 2006 (UTC) :I agree with the "de-merging" proposal. --[[User:GenkiNeko|GenkiNeko]] 04:23, 24 November 2006 (UTC) :: As long as there was fairly little information on the shrine, it made sense to merge them rather than the article of the shrine being a short stub mainly consisting of information that also could be found in the other article. Whoever has material for articles with some substance on both subjects, please go ahead. // [[User:Habj|habj]] 18:47, 25 November 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == different dojo == Isn't it the case that Aiki Jinja and Ibaraki branch dojo are located on opposite sides of the road, and that each side has a building with a tatami-floored training space (i.e., two dojos, even before including the Saito dojo and others)? A recent anon edit has changed the wording to say that ibaraki branch dojo itself is the one dojo contained in aiki jinja. Perhaps someone has a better source, but this [http://www.guillaumeerard.com/aikido/events/79-the-yearly-celebration-of-the-aiki-jinja-taisai] seems to show two distinct tatami areas used for aikido. [[User:Cesiumfrog|Cesiumfrog]] ([[User talk:Cesiumfrog|talk]]) 06:10, 30 November 2012 (UTC) — I did the edit. Please feel free to modify it to clarify: Erard's blog shows only one dojo. The other tatami area is the actual Haiden of the Aiki Shrine. If you look closely you can see normal straw tatami and the kamidana. This area is not used for training. About once a month the Haiden is used for Tsukinamisai (a shinto ceremony)and worship (no aikido) and once a year Doshu and Waka Sensei do a demonstration offering (hono embu) of aikido as part of Taisai. That is what the other picture shows in the blog. The demonstration is offered at the shrine much like the sea bream and vegetables on the kamidana. The only time the shrine has been used for actual training was when the dojo was damaged by the Tohoku Earthquake. The straw tatami were soon shredded and later had to be replaced . [[Special:Contributions/202.232.52.138|202.232.52.138]] ([[User talk:202.232.52.138|talk]]) 00:03, 19 December 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:31, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified 6 external links on [[Iwama dojo]].
